channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: MindsEye’s ‘Company Sabotage’ Mission Arrives in the Wake of Lawsuits
item.content:encoded:
MindsEye has received a new mission called Blacklisted, which allegedly provides evidence on company sabotage.

For those unaware, it was recently revealed that Build a Rocket Boy employees were taking legal action against the studio for installing Teramind surveillance software on their hardware. Just a day after this revelation, a new mission has arrived for Mindseye, supposedly providing evidence of sabotage and the names of the people responsible.

MindsEye’s Blacklisted Mission Arrives Next Week

A post by Everywhere NET on X (formerly Twitter) reveals the release date for MindsEye’s Blacklisted mission. It is scheduled to arrive on April 28, 2026, and the players will infiltrate, sabotage, and engage in espionage on this mission as they step into the role of Julia Black, a world-class assassin sent to Redrock City. Players will have to eliminate two key targets, and the process will involve infiltrating a criminal network, sabotaging a deal, and uncovering the bigger mystery.

That’s not all that is coming to MindsEye as new content. The developers revealed earlier this month that multiplayer is coming to Arcadia through competitive racing. Additionally, players are receiving a race builder that provides tools to create their own multiplayer and single-player games. Lastly, even more content is coming with the developers planning regular updates throughout the year; they will also soon provide console players access to a library of player-made games from the PC community.

In other news, MindsEye Co-CEO Mark Gerhard has alleged in an internal meeting that the company has caught those who sabotaged its game. Additionally, IO Interactive and Build a Rocket Boy’s publishing collaboration for MindsEye has concluded. Will you be playing MindsEye’s Blacklisted mission next week? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Fortnite receiving a Ben 10 collaboration. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Fortnite Drops Ben 10 Teaser Ahead Of Official Collaboration
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite has an incredibly extensive history of collabs with tons of different IPs, and it seems like another one is about to be added to the list, as Ben 10 and his cousin Gwen are officially coming to Fortnite.

The news comes from Fortnite itself, which released a short teaser video showcasing Ben 10 in action with the classic Omnitrix. According to leakers, the full collaboration will drop this Friday, April 24.

Ben 10 Cosmetics Heading To Fortnite

The teaser video released by Fortnite showcases a glimpse of what to expect from the upcoming partnership. As Ben flicks through the Omnitrix, four of his transformations are visible, including:

  • Stinkfly
  • Wildmutt
  • Heatblast
  • Alien X

Leaks suggest that Alien X and Heatblast will be two edit styles for the Ben 10 skin, whilst Gwen should get one for herself, though that remains unknown for the time being. If anything, it’s likely to be her Anodite form, though she does wear the Omnitrix herself in a one-off episode, so it’s all speculation for now.

As for other notorious alien forms, they look likely to appear as other cosmetics, including Backblings, Pickaxes, Gliders, Kicks and Wraps. However, now that the collab is coming, it could potentially open the doors to more skins in the future, including the likes of Four Arms and XLR8.

A full reveal is expected tomorrow, but in the meantime, check out the teaser for yourself down below, and enjoy your first glimpse of the Ben 10 collab.

Will you be picking up any of the Ben 10 Fortnite skins? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the potential ‘pick your plan’ formula heading to Xbox Game Pass, as well as the latest video game hardware sales report.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Video Game Hardware Sales Rose 69% In March 2026
item.content:encoded:
March 2026 was a nice month for video game hardware sales in the United States. In the latest sales data from Circana, hardware sales rose an impressive 69% year over year.

From March 1 to April 4, sales totaled $500 million, up from $297 million a year ago.

This was led by the Nintendo Switch 2, which led in both units sold and dollars for the month. The PlayStation 5 (PS5) finished second for the month in both measurements and actually saw a rise of 3% year-over-year.

Going back to the Nintendo Switch 2, Circana’s Mat Piscatella said that the console’s sales are 12% higher than the original Switch in its first 10 months on the market. Nintendo’s latest system also remains the “second-fastest selling hardware platform in US tracked history.” That data began being tracked in 1995.

  • Video Game Sales Data: Marathon Was One Of The Best-Selling Games In March 2026

Looking at year-to-date sales, the Nintendo Switch 2 is also at the top in both units and dollars, with the PS5 in second in both categories.

For 2026, video game hardware sales are up 38% compared to 2025. So far, hardware spending has reached $1.07 billion through three months. At this time last year, that number was only $777 million as consumers waited for the pending release of the aforementioned Switch 2.

Lastly, accessory sales remain even for 2026, but did rise 5% year-over-year for March to $252 million. That was thanks to a 180% increase in cases and organizer spending compared to March 2025.

What do you think about the rise in video game hardware sales in the last month and for the year as a whole? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Ubisoft canceling its Animal Crossing-inspired game Alterra. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Xbox Game Pass Could Get ‘Pick Your Plan’ Formula, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
According to a reliable source, Xbox Game Pass might be looking to become more flexible, with a ‘pick your own plan’ option being considered.

Xbox Game Pass recently experienced some huge changes, one of which will be appreciated by players, while the other won’t. Firstly, the price has been dropped effective immediately, and secondly, Call of Duty titles will no longer release Day One on Game Pass. However, now we are discussing future plans for the service.

Xbox Game Pass is Looking to Become More Flexible

A recent report from WindowsCentral indicates that Microsoft sources have informed Jez Corden of a long-term plan to make Xbox Game Pass more flexible. One option being considered is a “pick your own plan” feature. This feature would allow players to customize their subscriptions by choosing which services they want. For example, players could opt to remove features like Xbox Cloud Gaming or Fortnite Crew to lower the subscription price or add other services that better suit their needs.

Talking about Game Pass, another report from earlier this month reveals that Xbox is testing different offerings for new tiers. There’s apparently a TRITON tier in the works, rumored to feature only first-party games. Another one is called Duet, which is supposedly a bundle with a Netflix membership. What’s more, dataminers have also found that these tiers could feature monthly time allowances for Xbox Cloud Gaming.

In other news, check out the games that were added to Xbox Game Pass April 2026 Wave 2, and also all the games that will be leaving the service this month. What are your thoughts on Xbox Game Pass becoming more flexible with a ‘pick your own plan’ option? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Marathon becoming the best-selling game in March 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
